channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to know about Apple’s biggest-ever U.S. investment
item.description: This morning, Apple announced its largest spend commitment to date: A whopping $500 billion that’s set to be invested in American manufacturing, engineering, and AI efforts over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Grace Snelling, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic Unveils Claude 3.7 Sonnet: Faster Results and Integrated Reasoning
item.description: Anthropic releases Claude 3.7 Sonnet, a model that returns results faster and provides integrated reasoning capabilities, outperforming some benchmark tests.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The rise of the TV monitor: MSI joins the likes of Samsung and LG with a smart monitor that offers Google TV and even a remote control
item.description: MSI enters smart monitor market with 27-inch Modern MD272UPSW, blending 4K UHD visuals with Google TV.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SEC ends investigation into Robinhood's crypto arm
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has closed its investigation into the cryptocurrency arm of Robinhood without taking action against the online brokerage, the company announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fraudsters seem to target Seagate hard drives in order to pass old, used HDDs as new ones using intricate techniques
item.description: Fraudsters exploit Seagate hard drives, resetting SMART data to pass off heavily used HDDs as brand new.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How ‘lore’ became the internet’s favorite way to overshare
item.description: Lore isn’t just for games like The Elder Scrolls or films like The Lord of the Rings—online, it has evolved into something entirely new. The Old English word made the shortlist for Oxford University Presss’ 2024 Word of the Year (though it ultimately lost to brain rot).
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk's underground digital detective reveals all to NewsNation
item.description: NewsNation's Brian Entin has the first-ever interview with the tech genius using AI to discover government waste and corruption.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Comedians are turning to LinkedIn to find a new audience
item.description: Ben Sweeny, the salesman-turned-comedian behind that online persona Corporate Sween, says that bosses should waterboard their employees. “Some companies drown their employees with boring surveys and useless questionnaires,” he proclaims in a satirical video posted to LinkedIn a few months ago.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s latest iOS update improves CarPlay, but not everyone will be able to access it
item.description: A third row of apps has appeared on some user's infotainment systems following the recent iOS 18.4 beta update.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft gives Azure AI users a place to experiment with latest technologies
item.description: With the pace of innovation in AI accelerating, it’s important for developers to get early access to new tools and technologies. To that end, Microsoft has introduced a module inside its Azure AI Foundry service that aims to bring the latest innovations and research in AI to developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Norton boosts AI scam protection tools for all users
item.description: AI-powered scam protection is being built directly into Norton plans and covers texts, emails, web, and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: SEC, DOJ investigate CrowdStrike deal with reseller Carahsoft
item.description: US feds are reportedly investigating a $32 million deal inked by CrowdStrike with a government reseller to provide cybersecurity tools for the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) — products the agency never used and said it didn’t even purchase.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why a proposed ban on lab-grown meat is getting pushback from Nebraska ranchers
item.description: The prospect of banning the sale of so-called lab-grown meat might seem like a no-brainer in Nebraska, where beef is king, but some of the proposal’s staunchest opposition has come from ranchers and farming groups who say they can compete without the government’s help. Nebraska Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: A Brazilian Supreme Court justice has ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amazon concedes that Chime SDK makes far more sense than the Chime application itself
item.description: Amazon’s announcement on Wednesday that it is abandoning its Chime collaboration app, while stressing that it will double down on the far more successful Chime software development kit (SDK), was an example of Amazon being Amazon. It knows what it does well, and where to focus.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology
item.description: "Data is getting even more gravity in this space because the more data you have, the more accuracy these parameters get in your model," said Bill Vass. The post How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: This new AI tool helps Walmart’s merchandising team plan what’s in stores
item.description: Within Walmart, employees known as merchants make decisions about which products the company carries online and in stores, as well as pricing for those items.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple terminates UK data protection after government overreach
item.description: As of 10 a.m. (ET) today, Apple is no longer offering new users in the UK the chance to enable Advanced Data Protection. Essentially, anyone who now wants to enable the feature to protect their data against criminal or state surveillance or exfiltration will no longer be able to do so.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GenAI is my copilot? Developers weigh pains and gains
item.description: Developers are quicker than most to embrace generative AI tools as part of their workflow. That can mean enhanced productivity and easing cognitive burdens, but it also means learning new technologies while they’re still being built.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Everything we know about Apple’s C1 5G modem in iPhone 16e
item.description: There’s a lot to like about Apple’s newest $599 iPhone 16e, but one of the more interesting features in the device is the Apple-designed 5G modem. Apple has spent at least seven years developing the chip, but gave it just a few short seconds of time when it announced the smartphone this week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
